title: fagara pohliana
intro: Fagara pohliana, commonly known as the 'Chinese prickly ash', is a type of flowering plant in the Rutaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including the ability to stimulate digestion, relieve nausea, and reduce fever. The therapeutic actions of Fagara pohliana include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, which contribute to its use in treating various ailments such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Fagara pohliana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
benefits: Fagara pohliana has many benefits, such as its ability to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever reduction, pain relief, and the alleviation of inflammation. Its antimicrobial properties also make it effective in combating bacterial and fungal infections, promoting wound healing and preventing infections in people with compromised immune systems. Additionally, Fagara pohliana has been shown to possess antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases like cancer and heart disease. By harnessing the medicinal properties of Fagara pohliana, individuals can enjoy improved overall health, increased energy levels, and enhanced quality of life, allowing them to fully engage in daily activities and pursue their passions with renewed vitality.

Its antiseptic and antifungal properties can aid in wound healing, prevent infections, and reduce the risk of diseases. constituents: Fagara pohliana has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The flavonoids present in Fagara pohliana, including quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and protect against cell damage. The alkaloids in Fagara pohliana, including berberine and palmatine, exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which can aid in the treatment of infections and respiratory issues, ultimately improving the quality of life for individuals with compromised immune systems. Additionally, the terpenoids in Fagara pohliana, such as limonene and pinene, have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of infection.
parts: Fagara pohliana has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, roots, and fruits, which are utilized in traditional medicine. The leaves of Fagara pohliana contain alkaloids, including fagarine, and flavonoids like quercetin, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties. The roots of the plant contain coumarins, such as aesculetin and scopoletin, which have been found to have antimicrobial and antifungal effects. The fruits of Fagara pohliana contain xanthones, including xanthone and 2-hydroxyxanthone, which have been reported to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.
side_effects: Fagara pohliana can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing excessive sweating, dizziness, and stomach discomfort in some individuals. In rare cases, it may also cause liver damage and allergic reactions, particularly when ingested in large quantities or combined with other medications. Prolonged use of Fagara pohliana can lead to an imbalance of certain bodily chemicals, resulting in symptoms like insomnia and anxiety. Additionally, improper application of Fagara pohliana extracts to the skin may cause skin irritation, redness, and even burns.
